title: Eriogonum pilicaule
intro: Eriogonum pilicaule, also known as Mountain Buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America. The health benefits of Eriogonum pilicaule include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Eriogonum pilicaule is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Eriogonum pilicaule is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae. This plant is a type of buckwheat sagebrush, a name that reflects its native habitat in North America. Eriogonum pilicaule is native to the southwestern United States, specifically in Arizona and New Mexico. It is a shrub that grows to a height of approximately one to two meters. The classification of Eriogonum pilicaule also places it within the genus Eriogonum and the order Caryophyllales.

properties: Eriogonum pilicaule, commonly known as pinyon buckwheat, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems contain flavonoids, tannins, and alkaloids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential. Traditional healers have employed Eriogonum pilicaule for its purported ability to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
constituents: Eriogonum pilicaule contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.  The exact composition of its active constituents is not fully understood, but research suggests it may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Further studies are needed to fully elucidate the active constituents of this plant.
